name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 71 Saturday. **CF INDUSTRIES -3.1% TO $115.02 MAY 8 ON BLOWOUT EARNINGS PRINT — SELL-THE-NEWS AFTER +54% YTD RUN; FERTILIZER CASCADE TRADE TIRED AT THIS LEVEL DESPITE OPERATIONAL TAILWIND.** CF Q1 2026 May 7: EPS $3.98 vs $2.50 estimate (+59% surprise); revenue $1.99B vs $1.8B; net earnings $615M; EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization in Q1. Stock had run +54% YTD, +46% 6M into the print. Aftermarket -4.79% on classic sell-the-news + concerns about 'sustainability of current nitrogen pricing.' **What's NOT yet in the price:** Urea Yuzhny FOB $616.25/T (+6.71% 1D May 6, +15.12% MoM, +81.69% YTD); DAP $722.50/T (+0.35% week, +11.58% MoM); Hormuz handles ~35% of global seaborne fertilizer trade (farmdoc, Argus); Cape of Good Hope rerouting adds 10-14 days; **China sulfuric acid export suspension from May 2026 = structural phosphate bottleneck.** Net: nitrogen/phosphate fundamentals are STRONGER than CF's earnings already showed. **The smart-money position is rotating from fertilizer-already-up (CF, NTR) to fertilizer-supply-chain (BASF +1.5%, Linde flat, Indian fertilizer producers as proxy).** Limited crisis-attributable component on CF -3.1% (~10% crisis residual; ~90% profit-take); persistent fundamental tailwind from Hormuz/sulfuric-acid bottleneck not yet exhausted. **Reversibility:** limited from this print; but if MOU signs, urea moderates toward Argus base case; if Iran rejects past May 21, urea has room to spike +20% on confirmed durability of Hormuz disruption.

recent_signals.headline: CF -6.5% / NTR -4.4% — APRIL 17 PLAYBOOK RERUN: MOU 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES' = QATAR FERTILIZER NORMALIZATION EXPECTATION = SCARCITY-PREMIUM UNWIND.
